title: Validating and Normalizing HTML Anchor Link Attributes

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate and normalize HTML anchor link attributes. The function should take a string representing the anchor element and return an object with the valid attributes. The function should handle cases where the anchor element is missing the 'href' attribute, and cases where the 'target' attribute is set to an invalid value. Normalization of attributes should be applied as per the following rules: * If the 'href' attribute is present, it should be normalized to lowercase and stripped of leading/trailing whitespace. * If the 'target' attribute is present, it should be normalized to lowercase and set to '_blank' if it is not one of '_blank', '_self', '_parent', or '_top'. * If the 'title' attribute is present, it should be normalized to lowercase and stripped of leading/trailing whitespace. Example: Input: `Link` Output: `{href: "/path/to/resource", target: "_blank", title: "example"}` Note: The function should ignore any other attributes present in the anchor element.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the anchor element
statement.outputFormat: An object with the valid attributes
statement.notes: Normalization of attributes should be applied as per the given rules.

constraints.description: The input string is at most 10,000 characters long
The anchor element has at most 10 attributes

examples.explanation: Normalization of attributes is applied as per the given rules
The 'target' attribute is normalized to '_blank' since it is not one of '_blank', '_self', '_parent', or '_top'
The 'href' attribute is present and is normalized to lowercase and stripped of leading/trailing whitespace
The 'target' attribute is present and is normalized to lowercase and set to '_blank' since it is not one of '_blank', '_self', '_parent', or '_top'
